category_notes: Categories follow goarmy.com's six official enlisted job families. The traditional Combat / Combat Support / Combat Service Support / Specialized buckets are mapped: Combat = Ground Forces + Aviation (combat arms); Combat Support = Signal & Intelligence; Combat Service Support = Support & Logistics + Science & Medicine + Mechanics & Engineering; Specialized = Cyber (17C) and Special Operations roles.

mos.name: Infantryman
mos.description: As an Infantryman, you'll defend the country on the ground as a Soldier in an infantry unit, exposing threats, engaging enemy forces, assisting in reconnaissance, and mobilizing troops and weaponry as the ground combat force. You'll be trained to destroy the enemy by fire and maneuver, and to repel enemy assault by fire, close combat, and counterattack.

mos.name: Indirect Fire Infantryman
mos.description: As an Indirect Fire Infantryman, you'll be a member of a mortar squad, section or platoon. The mortar has the most power and range of any weapon in an infantry unit. You'll set up, load and fire three types of mortars: 60mm, 81mm or 120mm and employ crew and weapons in offensive, defensive and retrograde ground combat operations.

mos.name: Combat Engineer
mos.description: Combat Engineers work to help Soldiers navigate rough terrain and physical obstacles by clearing barriers with explosives, constructing obstacles and defensive positions, and detecting hazards. They provide their expertise in areas such as mobility, countermobility, survivability and general engineering.

mos.name: Horizontal Construction Engineer
mos.description: A Horizontal Construction Engineer operates heavy construction equipment like bulldozers, tractors, cranes, excavators, and road graders on some of the largest construction projects for the Army. They are responsible for operating tractors with dozer attachments, scoop loaders, backhoe loaders, hydraulic excavators, motorized graders and scrapers.

mos.name: Cannon Crewmember
mos.description: The Cannon Crewmember is a key member of a battlefield team that effectively engages the enemy with artillery fire in support of infantry and tank units. They are responsible for the operation of self-propelled and towed howitzer cannons, as well as ammunition trucks and a variety of other vehicles.

mos.name: Joint Fire Support Specialist
mos.description: As a Joint Fire Support Specialist (13F), you'll be responsible for gathering intelligence and enemy target location, operating radio communications, and determining where and how to deploy artillery in mission-based situations.

mos.name: Patriot Fire Control Enhanced Operator / Maintainer
mos.description: As a Patriot Fire Control Enhanced Operator/Maintainer, you're responsible for operating and maintaining the major parts of the Patriot Fire Control system. You'll act quickly and with precision to identify, track, and engage identified threats using one of the world's most advanced interceptor missile systems capable of destroying enemy air threats at a moment's notice.

mos.name: UH-60 Helicopter Repairer / Aircrew Member
mos.description: In this job, you'll repair and maintain UH-60 ("Black Hawk") Helicopters. As a UH-60 Helicopter Repairer, you will maintain and fix UH-60 ("Black Hawk") helicopters, making sure they are safe and ready to fly in hundreds of Army missions.

mos.name: Cyber Operations Specialist
mos.description: As a Cyber Operations Specialist 17C, you'll defend the Army's crucial and complex data networks and capabilities against foreign and domestic cyber threats. You will serve on the front lines of the cyberspace battlefield, enabling and executing both defensive and offensive cyberspace operations. Cyber operations specialists execute defensive and offensive cyberspace operations (DCO and OCO), and ensure the freedom of maneuver within the cyberspace domain while denying the same to adversaries.

mos.name: Cavalry Scout
mos.description: As a Cavalry Scout 19D, you'll act as the eyes and ears on the field, gathering information about enemy positions, vehicles, weapons and activity. You'll determine whether to call for reinforcements and when to order a retreat.

mos.name: M1 Armor Crewman
mos.description: The M1 Armor Crewman (19K) is responsible for loading and firing guns, and operating armored equipment to destroy enemy positions. The M1 Abrams tank is operated and maintained by Soldiers working in MOS M1 Armor Crewman. Skills learned during training include map reading and land navigation, operating and maintaining an Abrams tank in all types of terrain and weather conditions, and instruction on individual and crew served weapons including the M4 carbine and M9 pistol.

mos.name: Information Technology Specialist
mos.description: As an Information Technology Specialist, you'll maintain, process and troubleshoot military computer systems and operations, dealing with highly sensitive information crucial to security. You'll deal with highly sensitive information and need to have technical skills and aptitude for programming and computer languages.

mos.name: Network Communication Systems Specialist
mos.description: Network Communication Systems Specialist — part of the Army Signal Corps responsible for installing, maintaining, and troubleshooting the network communication systems that keep tactical and strategic Army communications operational...

mos.name: Multichannel Transmission Systems Operator-Maintainer
mos.description: A multichannel transmission systems operator-maintainer works directly on equipment that communicates through more than one channel and is responsible for the maintenance check of these devices, antennas and associated equipment. Multichannel Transmission Systems Operator Maintainers keep data moving across all levels of the Army's communication systems.

mos.name: Signal Support Systems Specialist
mos.description: In this job, you'll maintain vital signal support systems and terminal devices; the equipment that needs to consistently work in order for commanders to stay informed, track, and direct the movement of their troops.

mos.name: Military Police
mos.description: As a 31B Military Police, you'll protect people's lives and property on Army installations by enforcing military laws and regulations, control traffic, prevent crime, and respond to all emergencies. You'll contribute to combat power on the battlefield by conducting police operations, detention and security, and mobility support to enable force protection.

mos.name: Intelligence Analyst
mos.description: The intelligence analyst (35F) is primarily responsible for the analysis, processing and distribution of strategic and tactical intelligence, and is integral to providing Army personnel with information about enemy forces and potential battle areas. As an Intelligence Analyst, you'll be responsible for providing the Army with crucial and reliable information about enemy forces and potential areas of conflict, analyzing, assessing, processing, and distributing tactical intelligence, as well as creating, documenting, organizing, and cross-referencing intelligence records and files.

mos.name: Human Intelligence Collector
mos.description: In this job, you'll collect intelligence about an adversary's intentions, strengths, vulnerabilities, and capabilities and you'll share this critical information to help Army leaders better understand the enemy. You'll debrief and interrogate human intelligence sources, analyze and prepare intelligence reports, and screen human intelligence sources and documents.

mos.name: Signals Intelligence Analyst
mos.description: As a 35N Signals Intelligence Analyst, you'll examine and analyze foreign communications and activity to find intelligence, and you'll relay this critical information to senior leaders by producing strategic and tactical reports based on your analysis. You'll help place, camouflage, and recover surveillance equipment and identify, track, and analyze a target's operational patterns to assist leaders to formulate an attack plan.

mos.name: Signals Intelligence Voice Interceptor (Cryptologic Linguist)
mos.description: A cryptologic linguist is primarily responsible for identifying foreign communications using signals equipment. In this job, you'll use your skills as a linguist to identify, categorize, translate, and summarize foreign language communications from a specific location. You'll also operate and oversee signal equipment used in signals intelligence.

mos.name: Human Resources Specialist
mos.description: As a 42A Human Resources Specialist, you'll play a crucial role assisting your fellow Soldiers progress in their Army careers and ensure necessary support is provided to commanders across all branches. As a Human Resources Specialist, you will be responsible for providing essential HR support and services impacting every Soldier's career which includes accountability, strength, records, data, casualty, and readiness management.

mos.name: Public Affairs Mass Communication Specialist
mos.description: Public Affairs Mass Communication Specialists primarily write news releases, newspaper articles, Web-based material and collect photographs for use in military and civilian news media. Army public affairs specialists participate in and assist with the supervision and administration of Army public affairs programs.

mos.name: Biomedical Equipment Specialist
mos.description: As a Biomedical Equipment Specialist, you'll be responsible for servicing and maintaining all medical equipment with mechanical, hydraulic, pneumatic, electronic, digital, optical, and radiological principles. You'll perform calibrations and preventive maintenance, inspect equipment for serviceability, perform operational checkout procedures, troubleshoot and isolate malfunctions, replace and repair defective components, install medical equipment considering power and space requirements, and record all services performed.

mos.name: Radiology Specialist
mos.description: As a Radiology Specialist, you'll be responsible for operating X-rays and other medical imaging equipment used to diagnose injuries and diseases. The Radiology Specialist performs exams of the digestive, respiratory, vascular and nervous system with X-rays and related equipment.

mos.name: Combat Medic Specialist
mos.description: In this job, you'll administer emergency medical care in the field in both combat and humanitarian situations, and train other soldiers first responder procedures. Your training will allow you to serve as a first responder and triage illnesses and injuries to save lives, much like a paramedic in the civilian world.

mos.name: Motor Transport Operator
mos.description: As a Motor Transport Operator, you'll play an important part of the Army's transportation logistics team and supervise and operate wheeled vehicles over all types of terrain to safely transport cargo, troops, and provide advanced mobility on all missions. Motor transport operators are primarily responsible for supervising or operating wheel vehicles to transport personnel and cargo and are the backbone of the Army's support and sustainment structure, providing advanced mobility on and off the battlefield.

mos.name: Ammunition Specialist
mos.description: As an Ammunition Specialist (89B), you'll be responsible for the highly specialized care and management of ammunition, explosives, and their components, receiving, storing, issuing, and inspecting weapon systems, a crucial part of keeping troops safe. You'll perform stock control and accounting duties, and be in charge of receiving, storing, issuing, and inspecting weapon systems to determine if they need repair or need to be destroyed, including blowing up expired or partially used explosives and other ordnance in controlled settings.

mos.name: Wheeled Vehicle Mechanic
mos.description: Wheeled Vehicle Mechanic (91B) maintains and replaces brake actuators, batteries, starter motors, mechanical fuel pumps and hydraulic cylinders/pumps/control valves, services drive shafts, universal joints, brake shoes, water pumps and turbochargers, maintains and replaces cylinder heads, high-pressure fuel injection pumps and road wheels, and adjusts engine speed of governors/valves, carburetors, steering brakes, fuel injector racks and steering unit brake levers...

mos.name: Tactical Power Generation Specialist
mos.description: Tactical Power Generation Specialist supplies power needed to run Army activities and supervises and performs maintenance and repairs on power-generation equipment, internal combustion engines, and associated equipment.

mos.name: Automated Logistical Specialist
mos.description: In this job, you'll perform maintenance management and warehouse functions to maintain equipment records, inventory, material control, accounting and supply reports. The automated logistical specialist is primarily responsible for supervising and performing management or warehouse functions in order to maintain equipment records and parts.

mos.name: Petroleum Supply Specialist
mos.description: As a Petroleum Supply Specialist, you'll supply the Army with the fuel it needs to maintain a state of readiness at all times. You'll supervise and manage the reception, storage, and shipping of bulk or packaged petroleum-based products.

mos.name: Culinary Specialist
mos.description: As a 92G Culinary Specialist, you'll prepare and serve large-scale meals comparable to any major restaurant so that Soldiers can sit down and enjoy a hot meal in between training or mission deployments. You will be instructed in knife sharpening, cutting, scaling, and weighing procedures, and you'll also train to prepare a variety of foods ranging from breakfast foods, salads, and pasta products to beef, poultry and seafood.

mos.name: Unit Supply Specialist
mos.description: The 92Y Unit Supply Specialist is responsible for supervising and maintaining all Army supplies and equipment, including receiving, inspecting, inventorying, loading and unloading, storing, issuing, and delivering all supplies and equipment. A Unit Supply Specialist will operate an automated supply system and perform functions in property accountability, supply chain management, and procurement, while also safely securing and controlling weapons and ammunition in security areas.

mos.name: Radio / Communications Security (COMSEC) Repairer
mos.description: The Radio/Communications Security (COMSEC) Repairer (94E) performs or supervises field and sustainment level maintenance on radio receivers, transmitters, communication security equipment, controlled cryptographic items and other associated equipment.
